verdicts.detail: The documentation describes a complex multi-stage workflow that involves WebSearch, WebFetch, and a report_generator.py script that produces a full interactive HTML report. The exact stdout output of report_generator.py is not documented — the docs only show how to invoke it and describe the HTML structure at a high level, but do not pin any specific stdout text, JSON output, or even whether the script prints anything to stdout at all (it likely writes to a file). Without knowing the exact bytes printed to stdout, any test case would require guessing, violating the integrity rules.
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The documentation describes a complex inspection skill that calls live Alibaba Cloud APIs (RDS, CMS, DAS) and produces HTML reports whose exact content depends on runtime API responses. The check-write-operation.sh and inspect.py tools do not have documented standalone behaviours with pinned stdout outputs that can be verified without live cloud credentials and real instance data. No static, credential-free invocation with deterministic stdout is described anywhere in the documentation, so no test case can be constructed without guessing output.

The documentation does not pin any exact stdout output for any invocation. The tools produce HTML reports, process API calls requiring external keys, or run demos whose exact console output is not specified in the documentation. Without knowing the exact strings printed to stdout by any tool, no unambiguous expected_stdout can be constructed.
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

All commands either make live HTTP calls to Azure DevOps REST API (producing outputs that depend on external state), or produce error/setup messages whose exact format/wording is not specified in the documentation. The documentation describes what data is returned conceptually but never pins exact stdout strings, formatting, or error message text, making it impossible to construct test cases with unambiguous expected output.
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The documentation describes a Python library (importable modules) rather than command-line tools with documented CLI invocations, input formats, and exact stdout output. There are no documented command-line interfaces, argument formats, or expected output strings that would allow constructing test cases with pinned exact outputs. The tool filenames suggest Python modules/scripts, but no CLI usage, argument parsing, or output formatting is documented.

OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The documentation describes high-level workflow concepts and sample prompt patterns but does not pin any exact stdout output for any specific input. The extraction, formatting, tagging, summarisation, and graph outputs all depend on unspecified NLP/parsing logic whose exact textual results cannot be determined from the documentation alone. Without knowing the precise output format, field names, ordering, whitespace, or content for any given input, constructing a golden test case would require guessing, which violates the integrity rules.

The tool makes live network requests and returns dynamic search results from external services. The documentation does not pin any specific output values — the 'result' field is a runtime search response from a remote server, which cannot be predicted from the docs alone. There is no way to construct test cases with exact expected stdout without guessing the actual search results.
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The tool gov_chat.py makes external HTTP calls to a search API (深知可信搜索) that requires a valid API key and returns dynamic content from a remote service. The documentation does not pin any specific stdout output for any input — the output depends entirely on live API responses. There is no documented offline/mock mode, no fixed error message format for missing API keys, and no deterministic output that can be derived purely from the documentation. Any expected_stdout I construct would be a guess, risking false accusations.

The documentation describes the CLI tool's workflow and API interactions but does not pin the exact stdout format/content for any command invocation. The script communicates with a live external API (htmlcode.fun), and the documentation only describes what fields to report after a successful deploy — it does not specify the exact stdout format, labels, ordering, or wording used by the script. Without seeing the source code or example output, any expected_stdout value would be a guess, violating the integrity rules.

The SKILL.md documents a conversational iOS SDK integration skill and references a helper script `generate_excel_template.py`, but provides no documented CLI interface, arguments, stdin format, or stdout format for that script. There is no information in the documentation that pins the exact stdout output of `generate_excel_template.py` for any given invocation, making it impossible to construct test cases with unambiguous expected outputs.

The documentation shows CLI command syntax but does not specify the exact stdout output for any command (--check-db, --muscle, --equipment, or setup_db.py). Without knowing the exact format, wording, or content of the tool outputs, any expected_stdout value would be a guess.

OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The documentation describes a skill that calls external OECD SDMX APIs via HTTP and requires an API key. The tool scripts (call_api.py, config.py, tools.py) make network requests whose responses depend on live external services. The documentation does not pin any specific output format, data values, or even CLI invocation syntax for these scripts directly — they are Python modules meant to be imported and called by an AI agent, not invoked as standalone CLI tools. There is no documented command-line interface with predictable stdout that could be tested without network access and a valid API key. Therefore, no test cases can be constructed with unambiguous expected output.
OUT_OF_SCOPE_PROSE_SKILL

The documentation describes the scripts' purposes and general behaviours but does not pin the exact stdout output for any invocation. The scripts generate stochastic outputs (simulated annealing, random synthetic data), produce figures, and print results whose exact formatting, numeric values, and ordering are not specified anywhere in the documentation. Without knowing the precise stdout format, column headers, numeric precision, or even whether the scripts print anything at all to stdout (vs only producing files), it is impossible to construct test cases with unambiguously correct expected_stdout values.

The documentation describes the HTML report structure and CLI invocation pattern for report_generator.py, but does not specify what stdout the script produces when run. The generated HTML content is dynamic based on input data, and no exact output strings are guaranteed by the documentation. Without knowing the exact stdout (print statements, status messages, or silence), any expected_stdout would be a guess.

The documentation describes workflow steps and human-facing procedures, but does not pin the exact stdout output of sanitize.sh, validate.sh, or preview.sh for any given input. The docs only describe their purpose (sanitize keys, validate JSON, preview config) without specifying exact output format, success/failure messages, or output structure. Without knowing the exact output format, any expected_stdout would be a guess.

The documentation describes a skill that calls an external AI/API service to analyze frog skin images and produce structured assessment reports. The exact stdout output depends on live API responses, image analysis results, and dynamic data (timestamps, scores, enclosure IDs, etc.) that cannot be determined from the documentation alone. The documentation does not pin any specific output format or values for any concrete input that could be constructed without actually calling the API. All meaningful outputs (glossiness scores, wrinkle scores, scene classifications, alert levels, report content) are AI-generated and API-dependent, making it impossible to predict exact stdout for any test case.

The documentation describes a skill that calls an external API service and performs AI visual analysis on images. The exact stdout output depends entirely on the API response, which is not deterministic or pinned in the documentation. The documentation does not provide any fixed/mock output for any specific input — it only describes the schema of possible outputs (fields like stool_color_class, risk_level, confidence, etc.) and a table format for history listings, but the actual values returned depend on the AI model and API server. There is no way to construct a test case with a guaranteed exact stdout without being able to control or predict the API response.

The documentation describes shell scripts and a Python script that interact with external services (Snyk CLI, GitHub CLI, network access, repositories). None of the scripts have deterministic, self-contained stdout output that can be predicted from the documentation alone without those external dependencies. The docs do not pin any specific stdout format, success messages, or error messages for any invocation. All outputs depend on external state (Snyk scan results, GitHub API responses, repository contents), making it impossible to construct test cases with exact expected stdout.

The skill's output depends entirely on the contents of runtime data files (classroom_data.md, schedule_data.md, feedback_data.md) and complex multi-step scoring logic that is not fully deterministic from the documentation alone. The documentation does not pin exact classroom names, scores, or recommendation text for any concrete input, making it impossible to predict exact stdout without seeing the actual data files or source code. Even for the 'information not enough' case, the exact formatting/wording could vary. The only pinned output is the single follow-up question string, but the documentation doesn't make clear whether demo.js is a CLI tool that takes user queries as arguments or via stdin, and the interaction model is conversational/LLM-based, making exact stdout prediction unreliable.

The documentation describes output that depends on live Grok API responses (post content, trending topics, discussion summaries), which are not deterministic or pinned in the docs. The only deterministic outputs mentioned are error messages (e.g., XAI_API_KEY not set), but the exact wording/format of those error messages is not specified in the documentation. The output format is described as 'Obsidian-ready Markdown' but the exact bytes are not pinned. Without being able to predict the exact stdout bytes for any valid input, I cannot construct reliable test cases.

The documentation does not pin the exact stdout for any of the three tools. generate_koan.py output text uses ellipses and partial examples without full content. generate_image.py output (a uv command) is not fully specified. generate_tts.sh stdout is not documented. No test case can be constructed with unambiguously known expected output.
